Source_Species.reason: ZmLHT14 is studied in maize throughout the paper.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The study organism is maize, i.e. Zea mays.

Applied_Species.reason: Functional tests of ZmLHT14 overexpression were performed in maize.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The applied species is maize, Zea mays.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Methods mention both B104 and B73-329 as WT backgrounds for different materials; the paper does not explicitly state that ZmLHT14 overexpression specifically used B104.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: ZmLHT14 overexpression increased free amino acid concentrations in kernels at 21 DAP (Figure 5b).

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Free amino acids are the measured terminal metabolite class.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Discussion states amino acid accumulation is important for kernel nitrogen accumulation/grain filling, and ZmLHT14 enhances kernel amino acid accumulation.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Amino acid accumulation was measured in maize kernels.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper explicitly calls ZmLHT14 the amino acid transporter gene and names it LHT14, i.e. a lysine/histidine transporter family member.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: GFP-tagged ZmLHT14 localized to the plasma membrane in Nicotiana benthamiana leaf cells (Figure S4a).

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Transcriptome/qPCR results show ZmLHT14 down-regulated in zmccd8, up-regulated in ZmCCD8-OE, and up-regulated in ZmD14B-OE kernels at 7 DAP.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Kernel measurements were taken at 7 and 21 DAP, and field experiments are described in Methods.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods explicitly state Pro35S:ZmLHT14 constructs were generated for overexpression.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: The paper shows ZmMYB42 and ZmMYB63 bind the promoter of ZmLHT14 and repress its transcription, but does not show protein-protein interaction with ZmLHT14.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed approaches are in the paper: maize overexpression, free amino acid profiling, transcript analysis, and Y1H/EMSA/ChIP-qPCR/DLR showing MYB-mediated promoter regulation.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Figure 5 shows ZmLHT14 overexpression increased free amino acids and did not change soluble sugar concentration.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed point matches Results: SL-pathway-associated expression, direct repression by ZmMYB42/63, and increased amino acids in OE kernels including listed amino acids.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper supports utility for increasing kernel amino acid accumulation, but does not explicitly claim ZmLHT14 as a breeding target or mention nitrogen-use efficiency for this gene specifically.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The study validates in planta amino acid accumulation effects but does not directly assay ZmLHT14 substrate specificity in planta.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Methods explicitly list ZmCCD8 as GRMZM2G446858 in the GWAS section.

Source_Species.reason: ZmCCD8 is a maize gene studied in this paper.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The species is maize, Zea mays.

Applied_Species.reason: CRISPR and overexpression analyses were performed in maize.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The applied species is Zea mays.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Methods state the ZmCCD8 CRISPR/Cas9 and Pro35S:ZmCCD8 constructs were transformed into maize embryos of the inbred line B104.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The paper names CCD8 as CAROTENOID CLEAVAGE DIOXYGENASE 8.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: By name and function, ZmCCD8 is a carotenoid cleavage dioxygenase family enzyme; the paper cites CCD8 as an SL biosynthesis enzyme.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The paper explicitly states CCD8 functions as a plant strigolactone biosynthesis enzyme.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: ZmCCD8 is presented as part of the core SL biosynthetic pathway in maize.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: The paper does not explicitly describe ZmCCD8 as 'downstream/midstream' within the SL pathway.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: ZmCCD8 is clearly important for SL production and downstream phenotypes, but the paper does not explicitly define it as rate-limiting.

Primary_Substrate.reason: No specific substrate or 'carlactone pathway intermediate(s)' for ZmCCD8 is stated in this paper.

Primary_Product.reason: The paper shows ZmCCD8 affects downstream zealactone accumulation, but does not state that ZmCCD8 directly produces strigolactones or zealactone as its enzymatic product.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper supports that ZmCCD8 is required for SL biosynthesis and affects zealactone levels, but does not describe the exact catalyzed carotenoid-cleavage reaction.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: Results support that ZmCCD8 supplies an SL signal upstream of the transporter-regulatory module controlling sucrose and amino acid accumulation.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: ZmCCD8 affects kernel sucrose and free amino acid accumulation.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: These terminal metabolites are carbohydrate and amino acid classes.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper links these metabolites to grain filling, ear growth and kernel development.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: The paper states ZmCCD8 is highly expressed in root and ear during flowering and declines in the ear from silking to 3 weeks after pollination.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Overexpression is shown in OE kernels, and knockout lines are CRISPR null/mutant lines with loss of function.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Phosphate starvation was used to enhance SL production for root analysis, and kernel analyses were at 7 and 21 DAP in field experiments.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods explicitly state Pro35S:ZmCCD8 constructs were generated.

Omics_Data.reason: RNA-seq accession PRJNA978351 and GWAS using public MaizeGo data are explicitly stated.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are directly described in the paper.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show increased zealactone, sucrose/free amino acids, and enhanced ear traits in OE lines, with opposite effects in knockouts.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All four points are directly supported by the Results and Discussion summaries of experiments.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper explicitly says ZmCCD8 may be a target for breeding high-yield and nitrogen-efficient maize and identifies favorable promoter haplotypes/SNP associations.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results show 100-kernel weight unchanged in OE lines despite greater kernel weight per ear, and shoot growth changes were observed.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper supports an indirect role via SL signalling rather than direct synthesis of sugars or amino acids.

corrections.old_value: carlactone pathway intermediate(s) in strigolactone biosynthesis
corrections.reason: No specific substrate or 'carlactone pathway intermediate(s)' for ZmCCD8 is stated in this paper.

Source_Species.reason: ZmMYB42 is a maize gene studied in this work.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The applied species is maize, Zea mays.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Methods mention B104 and B73-329 as WT backgrounds, but do not explicitly assign ZmMYB42 knockout experiments to B104 in the provided text.

Regulator_Type.reason: ZmMYB42 is explicitly described as a transcription factor gene.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: It is explicitly called an MYB transcription factor.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Transient expression localized ZmMYB42 to the nucleus (Figure S4b).

Regulation_Mode.reason: DLR assays indicated ZmMYB42 represses expression of ZmSWEET10, ZmSWEET13c and ZmLHT14.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: Y1H, EMSA, ChIP-qPCR and DLR assays support direct repression of these three targets.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: These three genes are the experimentally validated direct targets.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: The paper shows UB3 binds the promoter of ZmMYB42 and represses its transcription, but no protein-protein interaction between ZmMYB42 and ZmUB3 is reported.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: ZmMYB42 is regulated by ZmCCD8/SL signalling and directly repressed by UB3; it is also down-regulated in ZmD14B-OE kernels.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: ZmMYB42 knockout increases kernel sucrose and free amino acids.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: The terminal metabolites are carbohydrate and amino acid classes.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper links these metabolites to carbon and nitrogen accumulation for grain filling.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: These phenotypes were measured in kernels.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: ZmMYB42 transcript is up-regulated in zmccd8 kernels and down-regulated in ZmCCD8-OE and ZmD14B-OE kernels at 7 DAP.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Knockout of ZmMYB42 increased expression of ZmSWEET10, ZmSWEET13c and ZmLHT14 in kernels at 7 DAP.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Kernel analyses were conducted at 7 and 21 DAP during grain filling.

Applied_Promoters.reason: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout is supported, and promoter reporters were used in DLR assays, but 'Applied_Promoters' is not a natural fit for knockout and the exact summary wording is not explicitly stated.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: Results support control of transporter-mediated sucrose and amino acid accumulation in kernels.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: ZmMYB42 functions as a negative regulator linking SL signalling to repression of assimilate transporters and reduced metabolite accumulation.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are described and used for ZmMYB42.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Knockout of ZmMYB42 increased soluble sugars and free amino acids in kernels.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each point matches the experimental Results for ZmMYB42.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The phenotype suggests utility, but the paper does not explicitly propose ZmMYB42 loss-of-function breeding/editing as an application.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper places ZmMYB42 downstream of UB3 in the SL pathway model.

Source_Species.reason: ZmUB3 is a maize gene in this study.

Applied_Species.reason: Expression and regulatory assays for ZmUB3 used maize/tobacco systems derived from the maize gene.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The gene under study is from maize, Zea mays.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: No dedicated ZmUB3 transgenic maize line/background is specified as B104 for core tests.

Regulator_Type.reason: UB3 is treated as a transcriptional regulator binding promoter GTAC motifs.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Although UB3 is known as an SPL factor from prior literature, this paper excerpt does not explicitly say 'SPL transcription factor'.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Figure S4 is said to include ZmUB3 localization, but the text does not explicitly state nucleus localization in the provided Results.

Regulation_Mode.reason: DLR assays suggested UB3 repressed ZmMYB42 and ZmMYB63 transcription in planta.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: Y1H, EMSA, ChIP-qPCR and DLR show direct repression of ZmMYB42 and ZmMYB63 via GTAC motifs.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: ZmMYB42 and ZmMYB63 are the directly tested targets.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Discussion/model explicitly state D53 interacts with UB3, citing prior data and the proposed mechanism.

Protein_Complex_Involvement.reason: The paper explicitly refers to the D53-UB3 module as a well-known SL signalling component.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: ZmUB3 is positioned downstream of ZmCCD8/SL signalling, and the model states D53 restrains UB3 activity when SL is low.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: ZmUB3 is part of the pathway regulating sucrose and free amino acid accumulation in kernels.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: These are carbohydrate and amino acid classes.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper links these metabolite changes to kernel carbon/nitrogen accumulation and ear growth.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The affected metabolites are measured in kernels.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: ZmUB3 expression was up-regulated in ZmCCD8 overexpression kernels and Figure 7e shows increased ZmUB3 expression in ZmD14B-OE kernels.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Expression/promotion-binding analyses are reported for 7 DAP kernels.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods state ZmUB3 coding sequence was placed in 35S-based vectors for ChIP and DLR assays.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: UB3 controls the MYB/transporter module that governs kernel assimilate accumulation.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: UB3 represses MYB negative regulators, thereby enabling sucrose and amino acid accumulation in the proposed pathway.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Y1H, EMSA, ChIP-qPCR, DLR, and expression analyses in ZmCCD8/ZmD14B backgrounds are all reported.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The indirect promoting role is inferable from pathway data, but UB3 itself was not genetically manipulated for kernel metabolite phenotyping in this study.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summary points are supported by Results, though the third is pathway positioning rather than direct phenotype testing.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a plausible implication, but the paper does not explicitly propose UB3 as an engineering target.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper indeed does not report a direct UB3-alone transgenic metabolite phenotype.

Source_Species.reason: ZmD14B is a maize gene studied here.

Applied_Species.reason: ZmD14B overexpression was tested in maize.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The applied species is maize, Zea mays.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Methods do not explicitly state that ZmD14B OE lines were in B104; they only say overexpression lines were generated by transforming maize embryos and provided by a center.

Regulator_Type.reason: The paper explicitly calls ZmD14B the SL receptor.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: As stated, ZmD14B is the strigolactone receptor, i.e. a D14 family member.

Regulation_Mode.reason: The model and discussion describe D14-mediated SL perception triggering D53 degradation and release of UB3 activity.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: Figure 7e shows down-regulation of ZmMYB42/63 and up-regulation of ZmSWEET10, ZmSWEET13c, ZmLHT14 and ZmUB3 in ZmD14B-OE kernels.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: These are the downstream genes whose expression was directly measured in ZmD14B-OE kernels.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: D53 interaction with D14 is part of general SL signalling knowledge and the model, but this paper does not directly test ZmD14B-D53 interaction.

Protein_Complex_Involvement.reason: The paper places D14B within the SL receptor/signalling module involving D53 in the model/discussion.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: ZmD14B is explicitly described as the SL receptor, so its input is strigolactone.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: ZmD14B overexpression increased sucrose and free amino acids in kernels.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: These are carbohydrate and amino acid metabolite classes.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper links these metabolites to grain filling and ear growth.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The metabolite changes were measured in kernels.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Figure 7a shows ZmD14B is overexpressed in transgenic kernels at 7 DAP.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Kernel grain-filling analyses were performed at 7 and 21 DAP.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: Results support that ZmD14B-mediated SL perception controls transporter-regulator expression linked to kernel sugar and amino acid accumulation.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: Overexpression was sufficient to enhance both sucrose and amino acid accumulation in kernels.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Overexpression, metabolite profiling, and qRT-PCR of downstream genes were all used.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The Results explicitly show increased soluble sugars/sucrose and free amino acids in ZmD14B-OE kernels.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summary points are directly supported by Figure 7 and corresponding text.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a reasonable implication, but the paper does not explicitly propose ZmD14B as a breeding/engineering target.

Other_Important_Info.reason: ZmD14B is the SL receptor branch directly tested in this study's maize kernel experiments.
